name: Technical English Communication
school: School of Social Sciences and Languages

questions.q: Identify the most appropriate taxonomy of technical writing for a marketing document (wearable health tracker case); justify with features + one more example.
Draft Minutes of the Meeting for a given Tech Symposium preparatory meeting scenario, as Recording Secretary.
Rewrite a poor smartwatch description into a precise, professional write-up with a suitable title.
10 MCQs covering tenses (future perfect continuous), conditionals, subject-verb agreement, active/passive voice.
Identify grammatical errors in a paragraph, underline and rewrite correctly.
Explain Taxonomy of Technical Communication — classify into 5 categories with definition + workplace example each.
Analyse a WhatsApp CAT-1 reminder message using the 5Cs of Communication (5 problems); rewrite it clearly and courteously.
(a) Correct 5 concord errors in a calibration-readings paragraph. (b) Complete 5 conditional clauses.
Reading comprehension on technical writing & verification — 4 questions (definition, actions, inference, example).
Define 5 words in 1-2 sentences: Textbook, Citation, Plagiarism, Summary, Evidence.
Explain the nature of technical writing vs literary/creative writing (purpose, audience, style).
Case study (NovaTech hybrid work reporting system): identify communication gaps + channel choice impact.
(a) Spot 5 errors in an online-learning paragraph. (b) Fill 5 conditional clauses.
Define 5 words in one sentence: Privacy, Leadership, Gratitude, Globalization, Entrepreneurship.
Reading comprehension on digital communication & workplace relationships — 4 questions.
Discuss precision, intent, and audience awareness in technical writing.
BrightMart business communication case study — apply 5Cs, identify 3 failures, rewrite core message.
(a) Correct subject-verb agreement errors in equipment passage. (b) Fill 5 conditional clauses.
Reading comprehension on Augmented Reality — 4 questions.
Define 5 words: Globalization, Sustainability, Ideology, Multiculturalism, Ethics.

papers.faculty: Dr. Shivshankar Rajmohan, Dr. Prajeesh Tomy

questions.q: Explain how branches of technical writing differ (peer-oriented vs user-oriented), with examples.
Anvaya Mobility case study (multi-stakeholder email) — analyse 5Cs violations + propose 3 revisions.
Section A: 5 concord errors in governance review paragraph. Section B: 5 conditional fill-ins.
Apply precise/clear writing to define 5 terms (50 words each): Blog, University, Hard disk, Chatbot, Mobile phone.
Reading comprehension on preserving indigenous cultures — 4 inference-based questions.
Elaborate key aspects and divisions in technical communication with examples.
Compose an email as a manager regarding updated leave policy & approval procedure.
Rewrite a poorly written rice cooker user manual applying 5Cs, specifying each error type.
Concord: 10 fill-in-the-blank sentences with correct verb forms (subject-verb agreement).
Conditionals: 10 fill-in-the-blanks across zero/first/second/third/mixed conditional types.
Explain 5 key characteristics of technical writing with examples.
Aarav Foods case study — apply business communication strategies; explain upward/horizontal communication.
Identify & correct concord errors in a 'Nature' paragraph (underline + rewrite).
Complete 5 conditional sentences (Type 0/I/II/III).
Define 5 words: Communication, Technology, Information, Management, Innovation.
Reading comprehension on hybrid/remote work models — 4 inference + fact questions.
Identify tense shifts in a biotech paragraph; rewrite precisely with corrections underlined.
Rewrite an AI cybersecurity passage in passive voice.
Write a Statement of Purpose (SOP) for MS Data Science at Oxford (200 words).
Prepare a 200-word abstract for a given research article on renewable energy adoption.
Civil engineering team case study — explain leadership strategies' contribution to team building.
Software dev/client negotiation case study — explain how negotiation resolved the conflict.
Define 'User Manual' in technical taxonomy with examples.
Yahoo data breach case — describe importance of 'correctness' and 'completeness' in technical writing.
(A) Correct SVA errors in a cricket-themed paragraph. (B) Correct tense errors in exam-malpractice paragraph.
(A) 5 conditional fill-ins. (B) Convert a Blood Donation camp passage Active → Passive.
Define 5 words: Webinar, Brandish, Inconspicuous, Orthodox, Philanthropic.
Climate change passage — 4 comprehension questions OR research paper parts short notes.
Write an Abstract for a remote-work-productivity research paper OR write an SOP for postgrad admission.
Write a 500-word Australian student visa SOP (academic background, financial support, return intent).
Explain best leadership style for a normal workday with example.
Belbin Team Roles case study (Thintext Solutions) — diagnose team composition problem + fix.
Marketing vs Sales conflict case study (NexaPharma) — root cause + mediation resolution.
Defend: formal vs informal report differs in structure, purpose, audience.
Discuss importance of each of the 5 C's of a technical report with examples.
(A) Correct 5 concord errors in a fleet-of-ships paragraph. (B) Correct 5 tense errors in an AI seminar paragraph.
(A) 5 conditional fill-ins. (B) Convert an FDP-announcement paragraph Active ↔ Passive.
Define 5 words: Perseverance, Harmony, Empathy, Discipline, ChatGPT.
Reading comprehension (Raghav/farmer passage) — 4 questions OR IMRaD format analysis.
Write a 100-word abstract for a physical-exercise-and-concentration research article OR an admissions SOP.
Critically analyze autocratic, democratic, laissez-faire leadership styles with examples.
Management student team case study — analyze team roles + role clarity improvements.
Consulting firm conflict case study (workload imbalance) — analyze causes + conflict management strategies.

name: Basic Engineering
school: School of Electrical Engineering / School of Mechanical Engineering

papers.faculty: Dr K Sathish Kumar et al.

questions.q: Calculate mesh currents for a 3-mesh resistive circuit.
Use superposition theorem to find current i in a mixed-source circuit.
Draw the Thevenin's equivalent circuit for a given network.
Series R-L-C (12Ω, 0.15H, 100µF) at 100V/50Hz — find Z, I, voltages, phase difference.
Explain construction of single-phase transformer with diagram.
Find current through 4Ω resistor using mesh analysis.
Find current through 10Ω resistor using superposition theorem.
Find current through 8Ω resistor using Thevenin's theorem.
R-L-C series circuit, 100V/50Hz, 10A leading current — find L, R, C, p.f., voltage across C, real power.
Discuss working principle of single-phase induction motor — why not self-starting; applications.
Determine current I using nodal voltage analysis.
Determine current i using superposition theorem.
Find current through 6Ω resistor (added between A-B) using Thevenin's theorem.
Instantaneous voltage v(t)=300sin314t across R-L-C series — RMS current, phase, i(t), voltages, p.f., P & Q.
(a) Function of commutator/brushes/armature in DC machine. (b) Transformer working principle. (c) Applications of DC series & induction motor.
Use node voltage method to find power dissipated in 6Ω resistor.
Determine current through 24Ω resistor using Thevenin's theorem.
Find current through 3Ω resistor using superposition theorem.
Transmission line load problem (4+j2Ω line impedance) — find real & reactive power at source, line, load.
(a) Working principle & construction of DC motor. (b) Applications of DC motor.
Identify incorrect dimensioning practices; redraw per BIS standards.
First-angle orthographic projections (front/top/side) of a machine component with BIS dimensions.
Projections of a pentagonal pyramid (40mm base, 60mm height) inclined 35° to VP, resting on HP.
Isometric view of a cone (50mm base diameter, 65mm height) resting on HP.
(a) Sand-casting process steps + cross-sectional sketch. (b) Extrusion process + applications.
Use mesh analysis to find vab and i0 in a given circuit.
Use Thevenin's theorem to find equivalent circuit & load current.
Use superposition theorem to find current through 23Ω resistor.
(a) R-L-C series with 200µF cap at 200V/50Hz — Z, I, p.f., power. (b) RC circuit voltage across R & C.
(a) BJT CE configuration input/output characteristics circuit. (b) Zener diode as voltage regulator.

papers.faculty: Dr. Vinoth Jebaraj A et al.

questions.q: Identify dimensioning errors in given figure; redraw per BIS standards.
Square pyramid (50mm base, 70mm axis) resting on HP, base side parallel to VP — top & front view.
First-angle projection: front view, top view, left-side view from a pictorial object.
Isometric view of a vertical cylinder (50mm dia, 60mm axis length).
(a) Types of pattern allowances in casting. (b) Consequences of neglecting shrinkage/draft allowance.

papers.faculty: Dr. Senthil Kumar M et al.

questions.q: Redraw a 2D drawing with corrected dimensions per BIS standard.
First-angle orthographic projections of a given isometric object.
Isometric view of a cylinder (40mm base, 70mm height) resting on HP.
Projections of a pentagonal pyramid (35mm base, 70mm height), one side inclined 45° to VP.
Illustrate sand casting process with sketch; list pattern allowance types.
Draw & correct dimensions of a given 2D drawing in Aligned System.
Draw orthographic projection (FV/TV/RHS) for a given drawing.
Pentagonal prism (25mm base, 65mm height) resting on HP on rectangular face, axis parallel to HP.
Isometric view of a cone (50mm base diameter, 70mm altitude) resting on HP.
Explain steps in sand-casting process and write defects in sand casting.

papers.faculty: Dr. Raghuraman D R S et al. (Closed Book)

questions.q: Redraw given figure and dimension as per BIS standards.
Draw orthographic views based on a specified isometric view with given front-view orientation arrow.
Hexagonal pyramid (25mm base, 60mm axis), base edge inclined 45° to VP, axis perpendicular to HP — top & front view.
Isometric view of a cone (50mm base diameter, 75mm height) resting on HP.
Explain extrusion manufacturing process with neat sketches.

papers.faculty: Dr Velu M et al. (Closed Book)

questions.q: List 5 dimensioning mistake types in an incorrectly dimensioned object; dimension correctly per BIS.
Draw orthographic projections (FV/TV/RHS) of Figure-1, first-angle projection method.
Hexagonal prism (25mm base, 65mm height) resting on HP on rectangular face, axis parallel to HP, perpendicular to VP.
Isometric view of a cone (60mm base diameter, 85mm height) resting on HP.
(a) List casting defects + remedies. (b) Explain shielded metal arc welding (SMAW) process schematically.

papers.faculty: Dr. A. Vinoth Jebaraj et al. (Closed Book)

questions.q: Identify incorrect dimensioning in Fig.1; redraw to full size per Indian Standards.
Hexagonal prism (20mm side, 60mm height) on hexagonal face, axis parallel VP, perpendicular HP — projections.
First-angle projection: front view, top view, right-side view from pictorial object.
Cylinder (55mm dia, 65mm height) on HP, axis parallel to VP, perpendicular to HP — isometric view.
(i) Purpose of pattern, core, riser in casting. (ii) Compare direct vs indirect extrusion with sketches.
Check functional dependence of u=x+y+2z, v=x-2y+z, w=-3y²+3z²+6xy+8yz+2xz; find relation if dependent.
Find Taylor's series expansion of f(x,y)=eˣsiny around (1, π/2), up to third degree terms.
Using Lagrange's multipliers, find highest/lowest temperature on sphere x²+y²+z²=10 for f=200x²y²z.
Evaluate ∬xy dx dy over region enclosed by x-axis, line x+y=4, and parabola x²=2y.
Evaluate ∫₀²∫ₓ^(x+2)(x+y) dy dx by changing the order of integration.
Find Taylor's expansion of √(1+x+y²) in powers of (x-1) and y up to 2nd degree; compute f(1.1, 0.9).
Find volume of greatest rectangular parallelopiped inscribed in ellipsoid x²/a²+y²/b²+z²/c²=1.
(a) Find du/dt via chain rule. (b) Check functional relation of u=(x+y)/(1-xy), v=tan⁻¹x+tan⁻¹y.
(a) Evaluate ∫₀¹∫ₓ^√x(x²+y²) dydx. (b) Evaluate ∬x² dxdy over region bounded by y=x and y=x².
Change order of integration and evaluate ∫₀ᵃ∫_(x²/a)^(2a-x) xy dydx.
Check functional dependence of u=x+y+z, v=x²+y²+z², w=x³+y³+z³-3xyz; find relation.
Expand f(x,y)=eˣcosy at (1, π/4) up to third-degree terms using Taylor series.
Open-top rectangular box, 256 cubic feet — find dimensions for least material using Lagrange's multipliers.
(a) Evaluate ∬xy dxdy over quadrant of circle x²+y²=a². (b) Find area between parabolas y²=4ax, x²=4ay.
Evaluate ∫₀¹∫ₓ^(2-x)(x/y) dydx by changing order of integration; sketch the graph.
If u=(x+y)/(x-y), v=xy/(x-y)² verify functional dependence; find the relation.
Expand log(1+x+y) about (0,0) up to third term using Taylor's series.
Thin closed box, one edge = twice another, volume 72m³ — find least surface area using Lagrange's multipliers.
Evaluate ∬(x-y)dxdy over region bounded by x=y² and x=y²/2+1; sketch the region.
Change order of integration in ∫₀¹∫ᵧ^(2-y) xy dxdy and evaluate.
(a) Find du/dt at t=2 for u=sin(x/y), x=eᵗ, y=t². (b) Verify ∂²f/∂x∂y=∂²f/∂y∂x for f(x,y)=log(2x+3y).
Expand f(x,y)=sin(xy) in powers of (x-1), (y-π/2) up to third degree.
Company manufacturing cost f(x,y)=5x²+2xy+3y²+800 with x+y=39 — find minimum production cost.
Evaluate ∬(x²+y²)dydx over area bounded by x²=y, x=1, x=2 about x-axis.
Change order of integration in ∫₀¹∫ᵧ^(2-y) xy dxdy and evaluate the integral value.
Show u=x+y+z, v=x²+y²+z²-2xy-2yz-2zx, w=x³+y³+z³-3xyz are functionally related; find the relation.
Second-order Taylor series approximation of f(x,y)=xy²+ycos(x-y) about (2,1).
(a) Find/classify critical points of f(x,y)=x³-3xy². (b) Find hottest point on ellipsoid 4x²+y²+4z²=16 with given temp function.
Evaluate ∬(7x²+14y)dA over region bounded by x=2y² and x=8, both integration orders.
Evaluate ∫₀^∞∫₀ˣ xe^(-x²/y) dy dx.
Evaluate ∫₀ᵃ∫ᵧᵃ x²/√(x²+y²) dxdy by changing to polar coordinates.
Evaluate ∫₀¹∫₀^√(1-x²)∫₀^√(1-x²-y²) dzdydx/√(1-x²-y²) by changing to spherical coordinates.
Show vector field F=xi+3yj+zk is conservative; find scalar potential; explain robotics application.
Verify Green's theorem for ∮(2xydx-y²dy) where C bounds ellipse 3x²+4y²=12.
RLC circuit (L=0.1H, R=20Ω, C=25µF) — find charge q and current i at any time t, given q(0)=0.05C.
Evaluate ∬√(9-x²-y²)dA over region bounded by circle x²+y²=9, by converting to polar coordinates.
Evaluate ∭(x²+y²)dV by converting to cylindrical coordinates, V: x²+y²≤4, 0≤z≤3.
(a) Directional derivative of f=xy²+yz²+zx² along tangent to curve x=t,y=t²,z=t³ at (1,1,1). (b) Show robotics potential field F is conservative; find scalar potential.
(a) VR haptic device force field — find work done along path y²=x from (0,0) to (1,1). (b) Verify Green's theorem for a triangular region.
RLC circuit (R=40Ω, L=1H, C=16×10⁻⁴F), free oscillations, zero initial charge/current — find q(t), i(t).
Use polar coordinates to evaluate ∬e^(1-x²-y²)dA, R in 4th quadrant between x²+y²=16 and x²+y²=36.
Use spherical coordinates to evaluate ∭x²dv, R inside x²+y²+z²=36 and z=-√(3x²+3y²).
Vector field F₁=(ax²y+z³)i+(x³+byz²)j+(cy²z+3xz²)k — find a,b,c for irrotational F₂; find potential φ.
Verify Stokes' Theorem for F=yi+xzj+x²k over a triangular pixel region cut from plane x+y+z=1.
Form ODE for series RLC circuit (R=20Ω, L=10H, C=5F); find current response given q(0)=1, q'(0)=0.
Find volume under paraboloid z=x²+y² above region D (upper half between circles r=1 and r=2), using polar coordinates.
Volume of material under paraboloid z=-x²-y²+9 over a circular wall boundary of radius 1.
Fluid velocity field F=(3x²-3y²)i-6xyj — check compression/rotation at P(1,2); find pressure if conservative.
Using Green's theorem, show flux around boundary = source inside, for F=(6y+x)i+(y+2x)j over circle radius 2 centered (2,3).
Find steady-state current in RLC circuit (R=24Ω, L=1.2H, C=1/90 F), Q=0.05, I=0 at t=0.
